next_steps: Perform comprehensive immunohistochemical staining on biopsy tissue (CD20, CD3, GFAP, Ki-67, EBER-ISH).
Submit biopsy tissue for specialized microbiological testing including GMS, AFB, and bacterial/fungal cultures.
Order CSF studies including cytology, flow cytometry, and PCR for JC virus, Toxoplasma gondii, and Epstein-Barr virus.
Perform whole-body PET/CT scan to evaluate for systemic occult malignancy or infectious foci.
Monitor neurological status closely and maintain dexamethasone for edema while withholding chemotherapy until definitive histopathological diagnosis is established.
Consult Infectious Disease specialist regarding potential for atypical opportunistic infections in the setting of long-term mycophenolate use.
